'''Nouata Pauli Pauli''' (born 4 August 1994) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] and the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. Pauli played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] and spent time in 2019 on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League. He also has played for the [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]] side.

==Background==
Pauli was born in [[Silverdale, New South Wales]], Australia, and is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/forget-fuifui-moimoi-young-gun-pauli-pauli-is-the-new-cult-hero-at-parramatta-eels/story-fni3ga7r-1226956432603|title=Forget Fuifui Moimoi Young Gun Pauli Pauli is the New Cult Hero at Parramatta Eels|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|accessdate=2016-01-12}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Hills District Bulls]], before being signed by the [[Parramatta Eels]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
From 2012 to 2014, Pauli played for the Parramatta Eels' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/p/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150705095446/http://nycdatabase.org/players/p/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=5 July 2015|title=P|publisher=Nyc Database|date=27 December 2012|accessdate=2016-01-12}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://rleague.com/players/Pauli-Pauli_3624/Stats/ |title=Archived copy |access-date=29 March 2014 |archive-date=28 October 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141028212522/http://rleague.com/players/Pauli-Pauli_3624/Stats/ |url-status=dead }}</ref> In August 2012, he played for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/assrl_2012_archives.php|title=ASSRL Schoolboys rugby league news, Australian Secondary School Rugby League, Australian Schoolboys|publisher=Ourfootyteam.com|date=|accessdate=2015-09-29}}</ref> On 5 July 2013, he re-signed with the Eels on a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/eels-announce-re-signing-of-youth-10271/|title=Eels announce re-signing of youth|work=NRL - Zero Tackle|accessdate=1 December 2014|date=5 July 2013|archive-date=28 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141028224821/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/eels-announce-re-signing-of-youth-10271/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2014===
In February, Pauli played for the Eels in the inaugural [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines|NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=SQUADS FOR AUCKLAND NINES|work=Rugby League Week|accessdate=1 December 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archivedate=15 January 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> In Round 4, he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Parramatta club against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]], playing off the interchange bench in Parramatta's 32–16 win at [[Parramatta Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2014/round-4/parramatta-vs-penrith/summary.html|title=NRL 2014 - Round 4|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=|accessdate=2016-01-12}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2014/03/29/round_4_nrl_late_mail.html|title=Round 4: NRL Late Mail|publisher=Parraeels.com.au|accessdate=1 December 2014|date=29 March 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.1eyedeel.com/forum/topics/pauli-pauli-to-make-his-debut-against-panthers|title=Pauli Pauli to make his debut against Panthers|publisher=Leyedeel.com|accessdate=1 December 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141028210815/http://www.1eyedeel.com/forum/topics/pauli-pauli-to-make-his-debut-against-panthers|archive-date=28 October 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> In round 8 against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] at [[Willows Sports Complex|1300SMILES Stadium]], he scored his first NRL career try in Parramatta's  14–42 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2014/round-8/north-queensland-vs-parramatta/summary.html|title=NRL 2014 - Round 8|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=|accessdate=2016-01-12}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/north-queensland-cowboys-thump-parramatta-eels-4214-at-1300smiles-stadium/story-fnjfzr2z-1226896917775|title=North Queensland Cowboys thump Parramatta Eels 42-14 at 1300SMILES Stadium|publisher=Townsville Bulletin|date=2014-04-26|accessdate=2015-12-15}}</ref> On 3 May 2014, he played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] under-20s team against the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] under-20s team, playing in New South Wales' 30–8 win at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/2014-nsw-u20s-origin-squad-announced/|title=NSW U20s ORIGIN SQUAD ANNOUNCED|work=Rugby League Week|accessdate=2015-04-10|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150402115014/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/2014-nsw-u20s-origin-squad-announced/|archivedate=2 April 2015|df=dmy-all}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/we-reveal-the-next-generation-of-stars-set-to-battle-it-out-in-the-under-20s-state-of-origin-clash/story-fni3fqyo-1226903806523|title=WE Reveal the Next generation of Stars Set to Battle it Out in the Under 20s State of Origin Clash|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|accessdate=2015-12-15}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/queensland-lock-luke-bateman-suffers-homophobic-slur-in-nsws-u20s-state-of-origin-victory/story-fnia3gmj-1226904424159|title=Queensland Lock Luke Bateman Suffers Homophobic Slur in NSW's U20s State of Origin Victory|website=Couriermail.com.au|accessdate=2015-12-15}}</ref> He finished off his debut year in the NRL having played in 17 matches and scoring three tries for Parramatta.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ny0xNjQ0LTQtLS0tLS0yMDc5MC0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-11-01|accessdate=2016-01-12}}</ref> On 8 September 2014, he was named in the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] train-on squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|title=Samoa train on squad announced|work=NRL - Zero Tackle|accessdate=1 December 2014|date=8 September 2014|archive-date=14 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141014081012/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|url-status=dead}}</ref> but did not make the final 23-man squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82434/default.aspx|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|website=NRL.com|date=7 October 2014|accessdate=2016-01-12}}</ref>

===2015===
On 31 January and 1 February, Pauli again played in the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines|NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/eels-2015-nines-squad/|title=EELS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-01-26|accessdate=2015-12-15|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150707105149/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/eels-2015-nines-squad/|archivedate=7 July 2015|df=dmy-all}}</ref> On 11 July, he re-signed with the Parramatta club on a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/eels-re-sign-seven-players-18135/#LQi9bGBx1MQPI5T0.97|title=Eels re-sign seven players | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2015-07-11|accessdate=2016-01-12|archive-date=8 December 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151208141424/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/eels-re-sign-seven-players-18135/#LQi9bGBx1MQPI5T0.97|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/the-parramatta-eels-have-re-signed-pauli-pauli-on-a-new-two-year-deal/story-fni3ga7r-1227438284695?login=1|title=Parramatta Eels Have Re-Signed Pauli Pauli on a New Two Year Deal|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|accessdate=2015-12-15|date = 11 July 2015}}</ref> He finished off the [[2015 NRL season|2015 season]] having played in 16 matches for Parramatta.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ny0xNjYwLTQtLS0tLS0yMDc5MC0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-30|accessdate=2015-12-15}}</ref>

===2016===
On 29 January, Pauli signed a two-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting effective immediately, after being released from the final two years of his Parramatta contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/knights-sign-pauli-pauli-20404/#hsAGhG1ewfykRqy3.97|title=Knights sign Pauli Pauli | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2014-08-15|accessdate=2016-01-29|archive-date=31 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131030042/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/knights-sign-pauli-pauli-20404/#hsAGhG1ewfykRqy3.97|url-status=dead}}</ref> In February, he played for the Knights in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/2016-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-official-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/91563/default.aspx|title=2016 Downer NRL Auckland Nines Official Squads|website=NRL.com|date=3 February 2016|accessdate=2016-02-04}}</ref>
[[File:Pauli Pauli.jpg|thumb|upright|right|Pauli playing for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in 2016]] 
In round 1, he made his club debut for Newcastle against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], playing off the interchange bench in the 12–30 loss at [[Robina Stadium|Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Laine Clark|url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/gold-coast-titans-grab-nrl-season-opening-win-against-newcastle-knights-20160306-gnbzkj.html|title=Gold Coast Titans grab NRL season opening win against Newcastle Knights|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=6 March 2016|accessdate=2016-03-07}}</ref> In round 12 against his former club Parramatta, he scored his first try for Newcastle outfit.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/knights-v-eels-five-key-points/tabid/10874/newsid/96499/default.aspx|title=Knights v Eels: Five key points|publisher=NRL.com|date=30 May 2016|accessdate=2016-06-05}}</ref> He finished the 2016 season having played in 15 matches and scoring one try.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY3Ny00LS0tLS0tMjA3OTAtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=|accessdate=2016-09-10}}</ref>

===2017===
Pauli was involved in a seven-car pileup on the [[Pacific Motorway (Sydney–Newcastle)|M1 Pacific Motorway]] on 18 January 2017, suffering a dislocated hip and was taken to [[Gosford Hospital]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/newcastle-knights-forward-pauli-pauli-involved-in-horror-car-crash-nrl-season-in-doubt/news-story/a6434860b5885e42b2adee8732a52210|title=Newcastle Knights forward Pauli Pauli involved in horror car crash, NRL season in doubt|work=Fox Sports|access-date=19 January 2017|date=19 January 2017}}</ref>

Whilst continuing his recovery, he featured for the Newcastle side in the [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]].<ref name="Newcastle forward Pauli Pauli never thought he’d play again after car crash">{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/newcastle-forward-pauli-pauli-never-thought-hed-play-again-after-car-crash/news-story/edc5c18b47aafe1f132adcf9b47adaa7|title=Newcastle forward Pauli Pauli never thought he'd play again after car crash|work=Fox Sports|date=2 May 2017|accessdate=7 July 2017}}</ref>

Pauli made his representative debut for the [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]] side in the final [[City vs Country Origin]] match.<ref name="NSW Country vs NSW City">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/9997/nsw-country-v-nsw-city-city-vs-country-2017#lineup|title=NSW Country vs NSW City|publisher=WWOS|date=7 May 2017|accessdate=7 July 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Kennedy|first=Chris|title=City edge past Country for final time|url=https://www.nrl.com/city-edge-past-country-for-final-time/tabid/10874/newsid/106489/default.aspx|website=NRL.com|date=7 May 2017}}</ref>

After continuing to build up fitness in the Intrust Super Premiership NSW, Pauli's season was ended in July after fracturing his [[fibula]] in a match.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/4785220/pauli-back-on-crutches-after-body-blow/|title=Pauli back on crutches after body blow|first=ROBERT|last=DILLON|date=12 July 2017|publisher=|access-date=11 September 2017}}</ref> The injury prevented him from playing another game for the Knights as he was not offered a new contract beyond 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2017/09/07/mitch_barnett_wins_d_915742400.af_news.html|title=Mitch Barnett wins Danny Buderus Medal|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|publisher=|accessdate=11 September 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170911121512/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2017/09/07/mitch_barnett_wins_d_915742400.af_news.html|archive-date=11 September 2017|url-status=dead}}</ref>

On 22 November 2017, it was announced that Pauli had signed a one-year contract with English [[Super League]] side [[Wakefield Trinity]] with the option of extending for a further season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity/pauli-pauli-tipped-to-provide-x-factor-for-wakefield-trinity-s-attacking-intentions-1-8871385|title=Pauli Pauli tipped to provide X-Factor for Wakefield Trinity's attacking intentions|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk|accessdate=23 November 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/2017/11/23/former-nrl-forward-heading-super-league|title=Former NRL forward heading to Super League|publisher=|accessdate=23 November 2017}}</ref>

===2019===
On 14 May 2019, Pauli joined fellow [[Super League]] team [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] on a months loan, swapping with Salford [[Junior Sau]]. He joined Salford club ahead of the [[2020 Super League season]].{{citation needed|date=December 2023}}

===2020===
On 17 October 2020, he played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat for Salford against Leeds at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===2021===
On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26–24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.{{citation needed|date=December 2023}}

===2022===
On 25 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[York RLFC Knights|York RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=York Press|date=25 Oct 2021|accessdate=25 Oct 2021|title=York City Knights pull off major coup to sign Pauli Pauli|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/19670107.york-city-knights-pull-off-major-coup-sign-pauli-pauli/}}</ref>

===2023===
On 13 November 2023, it was announced that Pauli would be departing York.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-super-league-forward-pauli-pauli-leaves-york-knights-for-another-club/|title=Ex-Super League forward Pauli Pauli leaves York Knights for another club|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

On 17 November 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal for the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Nov 2023|title=Former Super League & NRL powerhouse sees next destination confirmed following sudden York exit|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-nrl-powerhouse-sees-next-destination-confirmed-following-sudden-york-exit}}</ref>
